sanskrit:
कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते।
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते॥
transliteration: kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛtve hetuḥ prakṛtir ucyate | puruṣaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṃ bhoktṛtve hetur ucyate ||
literal_meaning: In the capacity of being the doer of effect and cause, material nature is said to be the instrument; the conscious person is said to be the instrument in the experiencing of pleasure and pain.
contextual_meaning: The body-mind system (prakṛti) supplies the hardware and software; the conscious self (puruṣa) supplies the user who clicks ‘accept’ on every pleasant or painful pop-up. Suffering is not inflicted upon us; it is chosen by the identities we keep investing in.
psychological_state: The moment the client realizes that every complaint about ‘how life treats me’ is a selfie: the body is the screen, but the finger on the shutter is mine.
trigger_scenario: You catch yourself doom-scrolling, feel worse, yet keep scrolling—then suddenly see that the algorithm only served what your history asked for.
anchor_text: You are the one who clicks 'yes' to every joy or pain.
